main.paragraph-main-1:
Public / Private Parts is a sensorial performance and video installation by choreographer and somatic sex educator Gerard X Reyes. It invites audiences into a world where intimacy expands beyond the screen and returns to the shared space of lived presence.

In a generation that fantasizes through screens on social media and hookup apps, the sensual body has been flattened into marketing images — available and desirable, yet increasingly distant and disembodied. Public / Private Parts asks what becomes possible when we begin again from connection and imagination?
main.paragraph-main-2:
Blurring the boundaries between performance, installation, and ritual, the work transforms erotic imagery into a collective, immersive encounter. Spectators move through cinematic landscapes of bodies, surreal sculptures, and intimate declarations, carried by a soundscape that unfolds across three sonic states: synthetic club pulse, breath and resonance, and ecstatic transcendence. Voyeurism softens into empathy, and critical distance gives way to mutual presence. Bodies gather in proximity; the audience becomes part witness, part participant in a choreography of intimacy.

Public / Private Parts proposes a queer space where sensuality is celebrated as a form of knowledge — reclaiming the body as a site of sensation, vulnerability, and possibility.

In doing so, the work imagines new ways of being together, desiring, and relating in an increasingly mediated world.
main.paragraph-thanks: Gerard X Reyes would like to thank all of the interviewees and dancers in Montréal, Berlin and Zürich who participated in the research for this work. Special thanks to Guy Cools, Julie Favreau and Andréane Leclerc for their guidance.
main.text-quote: “We learned care from exhaustion. We learned love from need, and that healing is a job nobody pays for. We still show up. We still call this work. This is the body of care. This is the body of love.”

press.spirale-magazine: By thus reinvesting the tactile and sensual dimension of their corporeality, the performers remind us that touching another person with respect is inevitably an act of reciprocity, and that this equality takes place in a form of sharing that alters the subject who submits to it. [...] The communion which is established between the performers and audience is supported by the musicality of the show: through the captivating rhythm of the soundtrack by Bendik Giske and Devon Bate, but also through the physical rhythm, the performers’ breath, which absorbs us and draws us into an immersive experience and practices to which they devote themselves before our eyes.
press.jeu-revue: Sensory masterpiece [...] It is singularly liberating. [...] Brilliantly executed. [...] Surrounded by performers in the flesh and by generous and fascinating virtual performers, Gerard X Reyes offers an undeniably sublime and important show. Liberating and destabilizing, this work offers us a view of ourselves. It takes hold beneath our skin, it settles there and it births life.
